name: puter-mcp-connector
description: Use your own Puter account's filesystem, website hosting, and serverless workers over MCP.
long_description: Connects an MCP client to a deployed Puter MCP Worker. Authenticates with your own Puter token (no shared credentials) and exposes tools to read/write/stat files, make directories, delete entries, host static websites, deploy/run serverless Puter Workers (which are built with puter.js and Puter authentication), and register launchable Puter apps. It also ships puter.js documentation tools so an agent can look up the exact API before writing worker or SDK code. The bundle runs a tiny local stdio<->HTTP proxy that forwards JSON-RPC to your Worker with your token attached.

tools.name: whoami
tools.description: Get the authenticated user's info (username, uuid, home directory). Use it to build valid paths.

tools.name: fs_read_file
tools.description: Read a file (UTF-8 or base64; optional offset/length).

tools.name: fs_stat
tools.description: Stat a file or directory (size, type, timestamps, uid).

tools.name: fs_write_file
tools.description: Create or overwrite a file (UTF-8 or base64 content).

tools.name: fs_mkdir
tools.description: Create a directory (optionally creating missing parents).

tools.name: fs_delete
tools.description: Delete a file or directory (recursive by default).

tools.name: fs_readdir
tools.description: List the entries of a directory.

tools.name: hosting_list
tools.description: List the caller's published websites (hosting subdomains, served at <subdomain>.puter.site).

tools.name: hosting_get
tools.description: Get a published website by its subdomain.

tools.name: hosting_create
tools.description: Publish a static website on a subdomain (optionally pointing at a root_dir).

tools.name: hosting_update
tools.description: Re-point a website at a different root directory.

tools.name: hosting_delete
tools.description: Unpublish a website (deletes the subdomain, not the files).

tools.name: workers_create
tools.description: Deploy a serverless Puter Worker from a JS file (uses the global router + puter.js); returns its public URL. Update by writing to the same file.

tools.name: workers_list
tools.description: List the caller's deployed serverless workers.

tools.name: workers_get
tools.description: Get a deployed worker (name, URL, source file) by name.

tools.name: workers_exec
tools.description: Call a deployed worker over HTTP as the authenticated user.

tools.name: workers_delete
tools.description: Undeploy a worker (leaves its source file in place).

tools.name: apps_list
tools.description: List the Puter apps the caller owns / can edit (name, URL, icon, aggregate usage stats).

tools.name: apps_get
tools.description: Get a Puter app by name; pass stats_period for detailed open/user counts over a window.

tools.name: apps_create
tools.description: Register a new Puter app (requires name and index_url, the URL the app loads).

tools.name: apps_update
tools.description: Update a Puter app by name (only the fields you pass change; new_name renames).

tools.name: apps_delete
tools.description: Delete a Puter app (leaves the site/worker its index_url points at in place).

tools.name: apps_check_name
tools.description: Check whether a Puter app name is available before creating it.

tools.name: puter_docs_index
tools.description: Load the puter.js documentation index (topics + paths) from docs.puter.com/llms.txt.

tools.name: puter_docs_get
tools.description: Fetch a specific puter.js documentation page as Markdown by topic path (e.g. Workers/router).

server_url.title: Server URL
server_url.description: URL of your deployed Puter MCP Worker, e.g. https://puter-mcp.<your-subdomain>.workers.dev/ (use http://127.0.0.1:8799/ for local `wrangler dev`).

puter_token.title: Puter Auth Token
puter_token.description: Your personal Puter token. Get it from a logged-in Puter browser tab: open the devtools console and run `puter.authToken`. Treated as a secret.
